title: List<T>
instruction:
`List<T>` is a resizable array. Use `Add`, `Remove`, and index access.

Run this.
successMessage: Count (not Length for lists). Add grows the list. Remove deletes the first occurrence of the value.

title: List methods
instruction:
Lists have many useful methods.

Run this.
successMessage: Sort sorts in place. Contains and IndexOf search. Insert inserts at a position, shifting elements right.

title: List with foreach
instruction:
Iterate a List with foreach.

Run this.
successMessage: foreach on a List is the idiomatic way to iterate. Count gives the number of elements.

title: Dictionary<K,V>
instruction:
`Dictionary<K,V>` maps keys to values.

Run this.
successMessage: Dictionary uses [] for both get and set. Add throws if key exists (use [] to overwrite). ContainsKey checks existence.

title: Iterate a Dictionary
instruction:
Iterate with foreach to get KeyValuePair items.

Run this.

codeChecks.message: Use foreach to iterate the dictionary. Access kv.Key and kv.Value.

successMessage: Dictionary<K,V> iteration gives KeyValuePair<K,V>. Order is not guaranteed.

title: TryGetValue
instruction:
`TryGetValue` safely gets a value without throwing on missing keys.

Run this.
successMessage: TryGetValue is the safe alternative to direct indexing. No KeyNotFoundException on missing keys.

title: HashSet<T>
instruction:
`HashSet<T>` stores unique values with fast membership testing.

Run this.
successMessage: HashSet ignores duplicates. Contains is O(1). ExceptWith removes elements present in another collection.

title: Queue and Stack
instruction:
`Queue<T>` is FIFO. `Stack<T>` is LIFO.

Run this.
successMessage: Queue: Enqueue adds, Dequeue removes from front. Stack: Push adds, Pop removes from top. Use these for task queues and undo history.
